summary: The winner for the March 2011 stock will prize is William Lee Nathaniel. title: Marathon runner
summary: CIRI shareholder Dustin R. Woodhead completed a personal goal by finishing the 26.2-mile Livestrong Austin Marathon on Sunday, Feb. 20, 2011 in Austin, Texas. title: Baby “Taktuk”
summary: CIRI shareholder Thomasina Morris (Throckmorton) and Wayne Nolte Jr. of Dallas, Texas, announce the birth of their first grandchild, Adrianna Ann “Taktuk” Kignak. title: Baby Mia
summary: CIRI shareholder Annette Piscoya and her husband Kooper Piscoya proudly announce the birth of their first grandchild, Mia Annie Piscoya. title: SCF collecting Elder stories
summary: Southcentral Foundation (SCF) will record Alaska Native Elders’ stories about Alaska Native history, tradition, culture and heritage. title: Want to know more about Alaska Native culture?
summary: The Alaska Native Heritage Center (ANHC) will be offering a mobile interactive tour for iPhone, iPod touch and iPad users in time for summer season opening day on May 8. title: CITC to host NYO
summary: Mark your calendars for the 2011 Native Youth Olympics (NYO) games! This year, the games will take place April 29 to May 1 at the Dena’ina Civic and Convention Center in downtown Anchorage. NYO typically brings together more than 600 athletes and 5,000 spectators from more than 50 communities during...

title: CIRI to distribute resource revenue payments April 1
summary: CIRI will distribute resource revenue payments to its at-large shareholders in the amount of $10.4635 per share, or $1,046.35 if you own 100 shares, on Friday, April 1. title: A word from the president:  Let’s be careful about how we link ANCSA and SBA 8(a) business development program
summary: By: Margie Brown, CIRI president and chief executive officer This year we will celebrate the 40th anniversary of the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act (ANCSA). It was a milestone event when President Nixon signed ANCSA into law on Dec. 18, 1971. The Act simultaneously settled Alaska Native peoples’ aboriginal land claims against the federal government and stimulated economic development, including construction of the trans-Ala...

title: CLDC, Weidner buy Arizona property
summary: CIRI Land Development Co. (CLDC) and Dean Weidner of Weidner Apartment Homes recently purchased The Peaks at Papago Park, a 768 apartment-unit complex, for $46 million. title: 11500 C Street completed on schedule, on budget
summary: CIRI Land Development Co. (CLDC) completed construction of 11500 C Street, a 40,000-square-foot building in early March.
